edges.description: Loss of enzyme activity allows undegraded sphingomyelin to accumulate in lysosomes.
edges.source: SMPD1 Loss of Function and Acid Sphingomyelinase Deficiency
edges.target: Lysosomal Sphingomyelin and Secondary Lipid Accumulation

edges.description: Foam-cell and neuronal storage drives organomegaly and neurodegeneration.
edges.source: Lysosomal Sphingomyelin and Secondary Lipid Accumulation

edges.description: Reticuloendothelial foam-cell accumulation enlarges liver and spleen.
Neuronal sphingomyelin storage drives progressive neurodegeneration.

edges.target: SMPD1 Loss of Function and Acid Sphingomyelinase Deficiency

nodes.description: Early enlargement of liver and spleen from sphingomyelin-laden foam cells, often by 2-4 months of age.

evidence.reference_title: Types A and B Niemann-Pick disease.
evidence.snippet: Type A NPD patients exhibit hepatosplenomegaly in infancy and profound CNS involvement.

nodes.description: Undegraded sphingomyelin, together with secondary cholesterol, accumulates in the lysosomes of macrophages and neurons, producing lipid-laden foam cells throughout the reticuloendothelial system and the nervous system.
nodes.id: Lysosomal Sphingomyelin and Secondary Lipid Accumulation

evidence.reference_title: Types A and B Niemann-Pick disease.
evidence.snippet: varying degrees of lipid storage and foam cell infiltration in tissues

nodes.description: Rapidly progressive psychomotor degeneration in infancy.

evidence.reference_title: Disease manifestations and burden of illness in patients with acid sphingomyelinase deficiency (ASMD).
evidence.snippet: NPD A is associated with a uniformly devastating disease course, with rapidly progressing psychomotor degeneration

meta.association: Biallelic loss-of-function SMPD1 variants causing acid sphingomyelinase deficiency

evidence.reference_title: Disease manifestations and burden of illness in patients with acid sphingomyelinase deficiency (ASMD).
evidence.snippet: Acid sphingomyelinase deficiency (ASMD), a rare lysosomal storage disease, is an autosomal recessive genetic disorder caused by different SMPD1 mutations.

nodes.description: Biallelic loss-of-function variants in SMPD1 (sphingomyelin phosphodiesterase 1) abolish lysosomal acid sphingomyelinase activity. The enzyme normally hydrolyzes sphingomyelin to ceramide and phosphocholine; its near-complete loss is the primary biochemical lesion of NPD-A.
nodes.id: SMPD1 Loss of Function and Acid Sphingomyelinase Deficiency

evidence.reference_title: Types A and B Niemann-Pick disease.
evidence.snippet: All patients with types A and B NPD have mutations in the gene encoding ASM (SMPD1), and thus the disease is more accurately referred to as ASM deficiency (ASMD).

evidence.reference_title: The Genetic Basis, Lung Involvement, and Therapeutic Options in Niemann-Pick Disease: A Comprehensive Review.
evidence.snippet: NPD type A and B are caused by mutations in the gene SMPD1 coding for sphingomyelin phosphodiesterase 1, with a consequent lack of acid sphingomyelinase activity.

nodes.description: Storage pathology produces early hepatosplenomegaly from reticuloendothelial foam cells and, in the neuronopathic type A, profound CNS involvement with rapidly progressive psychomotor degeneration leading to early childhood death.

evidence.reference_title: Types A and B Niemann-Pick disease.
evidence.snippet: Type A NPD patients exhibit hepatosplenomegaly in infancy and profound CNS involvement.
